prevalence.value: 15-30% annual incidence in children (episodes; mostly viral)
prevalence.source: Indian pediatric/primary care respiratory infection syntheses

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical assessment with Centor/McIsaac where used; rapid testing limited; antibiotics for suspected GAS when indicated

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High symptom resolution; stewardship needed to reduce unnecessary antibiotics

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Mostly clinical diagnosis; limited access to rapid testing and culture in many districts
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care for viral cases; penicillin/amoxicillin for GAS suspicion; rheumatic fever prevention remains relevant
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High antibiotic overuse and limited diagnostics; rheumatic fever risk drives empiric treatment in some contexts
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Availability of low-cost antibiotics and broad primary care reach in many settings
